questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The value(s) of $\theta$ satisfying the equation $\left[\sin \left(\cot ^{-1} \cos \tan ^{-1} \mathrm{x}\right)\right]=\sin \theta$, (where $x \in R$ and [.] denotes the greatest integer function) is/are
questions.solution:
We have $\sin \left(\cot ^{-1} \cos \tan ^{-1} x\right)=\sin \left(\cot ^{-1} \cos \theta\right)$, where $\theta=\tan ^{-1} x$
$=\sin \cot ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{1+\tan ^2 \theta}}=\sin \cot ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{1+x^2}}=\sqrt{\frac{1+x^2}{2+x^2}}$
Now for all $x \in R, 0 \lt \frac{1+x^2}{2+x^2} \lt 1$, then $\left[\sin \left(\cot ^{-1} \cos \tan ^{-1} x\right)\right]=\left[\frac{1+x^2}{2+x^2}\right]=0$
$\therefore$ The equation is $\sin \theta=0 \Rightarrow \theta=\mathrm{n} \pi, \mathrm{n} \in \mathrm{I}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider $f(x)=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{\sqrt{9-4 x^2}}\right)-\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)$. Identify which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: Number of solutions of the equation $f(x)=\ln (-x)$ is 2 .
Number of solutions of the equation $f(x)=\ln (-x)$ is 1 .
If $f(x)-k=0$ has a solution then number of integral values of $k$ is 4 .
If $f(x)-k=0$ has a solution then number of integral values of $k$ is 3 .
questions.solution:
$f(x)=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{\sqrt{9-4 x^2}}\right)-\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)$ Domain of $f(x)$ is $x \in\left(\frac{-3}{2}, \frac{3}{2}\right)$ $f(x)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{3}\right)-\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)$
When,$\begin{aligned} & x \in\left(\frac{-3}{2}, \frac{3}{2}\right) \\ & \left(\frac{-\pi}{2}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)-\left(\frac{2 \pi}{3}, \frac{\pi}{3}\right)\end{aligned}$ Range of $f(x)$ $\left(\frac{-7 \pi}{6}+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)$ Number of solution of $f(x)=\ln (-x)$ has only one solution $f(x)-k=0 \quad$ has number of integral solution $k=\{-3,-2,-1,0\} \quad \Rightarrow \quad 4$ value
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $a$ be a positive integer such that the limit $\lim _{x \rightarrow 1}\left(\frac{1}{x-1}-\frac{1}{x^a-2 x+1}\right)$ exists and is equal to $b:($ where $b \neq 0)$
questions.solution:
$b=\lim _{x \rightarrow 1} \frac{x^a-2 x+1-x+1}{(x-1)\left(x^a-2 x+1\right)}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 1} \frac{x^a-3 x+2}{(x-1)\left(x^a+1-2 x\right)} ; \quad x=1+h$
$b=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{(1+h)^a-3(1+h)+2}{h\left[(1+h)^a+1-2(1+h)\right]}=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{(1+a h+\ldots \ldots .)-3 h-1}{h[1+a h \ldots \ldots .1-2 h]}$
$=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{(a-3) h+\frac{a(a-1)}{2} h^2}{(a-2) h^2+\ldots \ldots .}$
$a=3 \quad$ and $\quad b=3 \quad \Rightarrow \quad a+b=6$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The value of $3 \sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\left(\frac{1}{\pi} \sum_{k=1}^{\infty} \cot ^{-1}\left(1+2 \sqrt{\sum_{r=1}^k r^3}\right)\right)^n$ is less than:
questions.solution:
$T=3 \sum_{n=1}^{\infty}(\underbrace{\frac{1}{\pi} \sum_{k=1}^{\infty} \cot ^{-1}\left(1+2 \sqrt{\sum_{r=1}^k r^3}\right)}_{\mathrm{J}})^n$
(1) $\sum_{r=1}^k r^3=\left(\frac{k(k+1)}{2}\right)^2$
(2) $\cot ^{-1} x=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & J=\frac{1}{\pi}\left(\sum_{k=1}^{\infty} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{1+k(k+1)}\right)\right)=\frac{1}{\pi}\left(\sum_{k=1}^{\infty} \tan ^{-1}(k+1)-\tan ^{-1} k\right) \\ & \frac{1}{\pi}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=\frac{1}{4}\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad T=3 \sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)^n$
Infinite G.P. $=3\left(\frac{1 / 4}{1-(1 / 4)}\right)=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Let $f(x)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x^2}\right), g(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^2}{1+x^2}\right)$ and $h(x)=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1-x^2}\right)$, then:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f(x)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x^2}\right)= \begin{cases}2 \tan ^1 x, & -1 \leq x \leq 1 \\ \pi-2 \tan ^{-1} x, & x \geq 1 \\ -\pi-2 \tan ^{-1} x, & x \leq-1\end{cases} \\ & g(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^2}{1+x^2}\right)= \begin{cases}2 \tan ^{-1} x, & x \geq 0 \\ -2 \tan ^{-1} x, & x < 0\end{cases} \\ & h(x)=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x^2}\right)= \begin{cases}\pi+2 \tan ^1 x, & x < -1 \\ 2 \tan ^{-1} x, & -1 < x < 1 \\ 2 \tan ^{-1} x-\pi, & x>1\end{cases} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The differential coefficient of $\sin ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{t}}{\sqrt{1+\mathrm{t}^2}}$ w.r.t. $\cos ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{1+\mathrm{t}^2}}$ is
questions.solution:
$y=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{t}{\sqrt{1+t^2}}\right)$, $z=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{1+t^2}}\right)$
$\because-1 \lt \frac{t}{\sqrt{1+t^2}} \lt 1$  and $0 \lt \left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{1+\mathrm{t}^2}}\right) \leq 1 \forall \mathrm{t} \in \mathrm{R}$
let $\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{t}{\sqrt{1+t^2}}\right)=0$ $\Rightarrow \quad \theta \in(-\pi / 2, \pi / 2)$
$\frac{t}{\sqrt{1+t^2}}=\sin \theta$ $\Rightarrow \cos \theta=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1+t^2}}$
(i) if $\theta \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$, then
$y=\theta, z=\theta$
$\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d z}=1$
(ii) if $\quad \theta \in\left(-\frac{\pi}{2}, 0\right)$, then
$y=\theta, z=-\theta$
$\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d z}=-1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $S_n=\sum_{k=1}^n \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{k(k+1)+1}\right)$ for positive integers $n \in N$, then:
options: the value of $S_{10}$ is equal to $\frac{\pi}{4}-\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{11}\right)$.
the value of $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} S_n$ is equal to $\frac{\pi}{2}$.
the value of $5+\sum_{n=1}^{62} \frac{1+\tan S_n}{1-\tan S_n}$ is equal to 2020
the value of $S_5$ is equal to $\tan ^{-1}(6)-\frac{\pi}{4}$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & S_n=\sum_{k=1}^n \tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{k(k+1)+1} \\ & S_n=\sum_{k=1}^n \tan ^{-1} \frac{\frac{1}{k(k+1)}}{1+\frac{1}{k(k+1)}} \\ & S_n=\sum_{k=1}^n \tan ^{-1} \frac{\frac{1}{k}-\frac{1}{k+1}}{1+\frac{1}{k(k+1)}} \\ & S_n=\sum_{k=1}^n\left(\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{k}-\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{k+1}\right)\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} S_n=\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{1}-\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{2}+\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{2}-\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{3}+\ldots \ldots .+\tan ^{-1} & \frac{1}{n-1}-\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{n} \\ & +\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{n}-\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{n+1}\end{aligned}$
$S_n=\tan ^{-1} 1-\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{n+1}$
$\therefore \quad 5+\sum_{n=1}^{62} \frac{1+\tan S_n}{1-\tan S_n}=5+\sum_{n=1}^{62} \tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}+S_n\right)=5+\sum_{n=1}^{62} \tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{\pi}{4}-\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{n+1}\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & =5+\sum_{n=1}^{62} \tan \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{n+1}\right)=5+\sum_{n=1}^{62} \cot \left(\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{n+1}\right) \\ & =5+\sum_{n=1}^{62} \frac{1}{\tan \left(\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{n+1}\right)}=5+\sum_{n=1}^{62} \frac{1}{\frac{1}{n+1}}=5+\sum_{n=1}^{62}(n+1) \\ & =4+(1+2+3+\ldots . .+63)=4+\frac{63 \cdot 64}{2}=4+2016=2020\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
